ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 / OceanLotus DLL side-loading via signed third-party binary relocated to user-writable path
ucs.description: APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty / Canvas Cyclone / BISMUTH) is famous for dropping a legitimate signed binary (Google Updater, Symantec RasTls, McAfee mcoemcpy, Sysinternals/Adobe utilities, etc.) into a user-writable directory together with a trojanised DLL that the binary will side-load (KerrDown, Denis, Cobalt Strike stager). This UC catches the side-load by surfacing those vendor binaries when they execute from non-default paths such as %APPDATA%, %ProgramData%, %Public% or %TEMP% — the exact tradecraft documented in the Cybereason Cobalt Kitty and ESET Bismuth reports.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("GoogleUpdate.exe","RasTls.exe","rastlsc.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","McVsShld.exe","goopdate.exe","FFViewer.exe","DTHelper.exe","wsc.exe","sysprep.exe","AcroRd32.exe","WerFault.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Tasks\\*") AND Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT32 DLL side-loading: signed vendor binary executing from user-writable path,
// then loading a co-resident DLL whose hash differs from the loader.
let _apt32_sideload_binaries = dynamic([
    "googleupdate.exe","rastls.exe","rastlsc.exe","mcoemcpy.exe",
    "mcvsshld.exe","goopdate.exe","ffviewer.exe","dthelper.exe",
    "wsc.exe","sysprep.exe","acrord32.exe","werfault.exe"
]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([
    @"\appdata\",@"\programdata\",@"\users\public\",
    @"\temp\",@"\perflogs\",@"\windows\tasks\"
]);
let LoaderRuns =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"                          // exclude machine accts
    | where FileName in~ (_apt32_sideload_binaries)
    | where tolower(FolderPath) has_any (_user_writable)        // not Program Files
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LoaderName = FileName, LoaderPath = FolderPath,
              LoaderSha256 = SHA256, LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              LoaderProcId = ProcessId;
// pivot to image loads from the same folder by the same loader process
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| join kind=inner LoaderRuns on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId,
                                $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.LoaderProcId
| where tolower(FolderPath) == tolower(LoaderPath)              // DLL co-resident
| where SHA256 != LoaderSha256
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LoaderName, LoaderPath, LoaderSha256, LoaderCmd,
          SideLoadedDll = FileName, SideLoadedDllSha256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel-side: Defender doesn't ship DeviceImageLoadEvents to Sentinel by default,
// so we anchor on the unmistakable behaviour — a known APT32 carrier binary
// running from a user-writable directory (4688 / Sysmon EID 1).
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith @"\GoogleUpdate.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\RasTls.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\rastlsc.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\mcoemcpy.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\McVsShld.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\goopdate.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\FFViewer.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\DTHelper.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\wsc.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\sysprep.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith @"\WerFault.exe")
| where NewProcessName has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\",
                                 @"\Users\Public\", @"\Temp\",
                                 @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Tasks\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus DLL Side-Loading via Signed Binary in User-Writable Path
id: 7b1a32d0-3c54-4cf2-9f9e-ap32sideload01
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty / Canvas Cyclone / BISMUTH)
  DLL side-loading tradecraft where the actor relocates a legitimate signed
  third-party binary (GoogleUpdate, RasTls, mcoemcpy, goopdate, FFViewer,
  DTHelper, etc.) to a user-writable directory in order to load a malicious
  same-folder DLL (KerrDown / Denis / Cobalt Strike stager).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/2020/11/oceanlotus-bismuth-cryptominer/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\RasTls.exe'
      - '\rastlsc.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\McVsShld.exe'
      - '\goopdate.exe'
      - '\FFViewer.exe'
      - '\DTHelper.exe'
      - '\wsc.exe'
      - '\sysprep.exe'
      - '\WerFault.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Tasks\'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Software-installer extraction staging in %TEMP% (rare for these specific binaries)
  - Out-of-band patcher running from %ProgramData%
level: high
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.g0050
ucs.rationale: Generic side-loading detections fire on every legit installer. This UC narrows to the *specific signed-binary names* documented across APT32 campaigns (Cobalt Kitty: RasTls/mcoemcpy/goopdate/FFViewer/DTHelper; KerrDown: GoogleUpdate; BISMUTH: WerFault/AcroRd32) and additionally requires the binary to be running from a non-Program-Files location — the structural condition that makes it side-loading
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT32 Cobalt Kitty scheduled-task persistence with obfuscated PowerShell payload
ucs.description: APT32's Cobalt Kitty operation registered scheduled tasks under disguised names (e.g. Microsoft\Windows\NlaSvc\WPDBusEnumRoot, Microsoft\Windows\Multimedia\SystemSoundsService) that invoke PowerShell with -EncodedCommand / -nop -w hidden / FromBase64String to launch Cobalt Strike beacons or Denis backdoor stagers. This UC hunts schtasks.exe creating tasks whose action is an obfuscated PowerShell or rundll32 javascript: invocation — the canonical APT32 persistence shape.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*create*" AND (Processes.process="*EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="* -enc *" OR Processes.process="* -ec *" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-w hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-WindowStyle hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-nop*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*" OR Processes.process="*javascript:*" OR Processes.process="*rundll32*javascript*") AND Processes.user!="*$" AND Processes.user!="SYSTEM" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT32 Cobalt-Kitty scheduled-task persistence: schtasks.exe creating a task
// whose action is an obfuscated PowerShell / rundll32-javascript launcher.
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","-create","/Create")
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where cmd has_any ("powershell","pwsh","rundll32")
| where cmd has_any (
    "-encodedcommand"," -enc "," -ec ","frombase64string",
    "-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-nop ","-noni",
    "iex ","invoke-expression","javascript:",
    "downloadstring","downloadfile","new-object net.webclient")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentBin = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel port — anchored on 4688 process-create.
// CommandLine collection requires GPO 'Include command line in process creation events'.
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where NewProcessName endswith @"\schtasks.exe"
| extend cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| where cmd has_any ("/create","-create")
| where cmd has_any ("powershell","pwsh","rundll32")
| where cmd has_any (
    "-encodedcommand"," -enc "," -ec ","frombase64string",
    "-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-nop ","-noni",
    "iex ","invoke-expression","javascript:",
    "downloadstring","downloadfile","new-object net.webclient")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT32 OceanLotus Scheduled-Task Persistence with Obfuscated PowerShell Action
id: 9a4f12bd-2d77-4e9c-abc1-ap32coba1tkit
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects APT32 (OceanLotus / Cobalt Kitty / BISMUTH) persistence via
  schtasks.exe registering a task whose action is an obfuscated PowerShell
  or rundll32-javascript launcher. Documented in the Cybereason Cobalt Kitty
  report as the actor's primary persistence mechanism for Cobalt Strike
  beacons and the Denis backdoor.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0050/
  - https://www.cybereason.com/blog/operation-cobalt-kitty-apt
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_schtasks:
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
  selection_create:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/create'
      - '-create'
  selection_launcher:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'powershell'
      - 'pwsh'
      - 'rundll32'
  selection_obfuscation:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -ec '
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - '-w hidden'
      - '-WindowStyle hidden'
      - '-nop '
      - '-noni'
      - 'IEX '
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'javascript:'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'New-Object Net.WebClient'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_schtasks and selection_create and selection_launcher and selection_obfuscation and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned admin-driven scheduled jobs that legitimately use -EncodedCommand
  - Software-deployment systems (SCCM/Intune) launching obfuscated PS via tasks
level: high
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.t1036.004
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0050
ucs.rationale: Rather than alerting on every schtasks /create (way too noisy), this UC requires three concurrent conditions that mirror APT32's documented Cobalt Kitty chain: (a) task creation via schtasks, (b) the action invokes PowerShell or rundll32, (c) the action is obfuscated (EncodedCommand / hidden window / IEX / FromBase64String / javascript:). That tri-condition is what APT32 looks like in their incide

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
